faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Greenburgh home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or other natural fiber clothing, webbing in closet corners, silken tubes or casings on fabrics, adult moths flying near wardrobes (especially at night), and shedding or damaged upholstery. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on affected items or see adult moths resting on walls near closets or storage areas.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Greenburgh?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home or business, the number of rooms requiring treatment, and whether follow-up visits are needed. Larger homes with extensive closet systems or significant infestations in multiple areas will require more comprehensive treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes for all Greenburgh properties.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets and storage areas, washing or dry-cleaning woolens before storage, using cedar products or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, storing off-season clothing in airtight containers, maintaining proper ventilation in closets, and scheduling periodic inspections. Our technicians provide customized prevention strategies based on your specific Greenburgh property and storage needs.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in Greenburgh?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and integrated pest management techniques that are safe for families and pets when applied by our licensed professionals. We offer eco-friendly treatment options and provide clear instructions for any necessary precautions during and after service. Our methods target clothes moths specifically while minimizing any impact on your household, making them ideal for Greenburgh homes with children and pets.
